Application Profile Configuration provides a quick method of BIOS knob tuning accordingly to application. It's based on benchmark tests and may be not suitable to all workloads. You can still override the options.

Full Mirror Mode will set entire 1LM memory in system to be mirrored, consequently reducing the memory capacity by half. Partial Mirror Mode will enable the required size of memory to be mirrored. If rank sparing is enabled partial mirroring will not take effect. Enabling any type of Mirror Mode will disable XPT Prefetch.
Enable Mirror on entire memory for TAD0
Imitate behavior of UEFI based Address Range Mirror with setup option
Enter the percentage to be mirrored in basis points. 12.75% is represented 1275

Attributes.HelpText: Disable: Hardware chooses a P-state based on OS Request (Legacy P-States)  Native Mode:Hardware chooses a P-state based on OS guidance  Out of Band Mode:Hardware autonomously chooses a P-state (no OS guidance)  Native Mode with No Legacy Support:same as Native Mode but only support new ACPI specification.

A parity bit can be sent with the data bits to detect some transmission errors. Even: parity bit is 0 if the num of 1's in the data bits is even. Odd: parity bit is 0 if num of 1's in the data bits is odd.  Mark: parity bit is always 1. Space: Parity bit is always 0. Mark and Space Parity do not allow for error detection. They can be used as an additional data bit.
Data Bits
Stop bits indicate the end of a serial data packet. (A start bit indicates the beginning). The standard setting is 1 stop bit. Communication with slow devices may require more than 1 stop bit.

The altitude of the platform location above the sea level, expressed in meters. The hex number is decoded as 2's complement signed integer.  Provide the 8000h value if the altitude is unknown.

Attributes.DisplayName: 100 Series PMem Average Power Budget (in mW)
Attributes.HelpText: Sets the power management policy for average power (must be an increment of 250 mW). Warning: Average power range depends on NVDIMM size. For 128GB range is 10..15 W, for 256/512GB range is 12..18 W. If value out of range is set, it is silently corrected.

Selects the number of hours (1-24) required to complete full scrub. A value of zero means auto!

Select Static Turbo policy that the BIOS will apply.Frequency ranges from selected frequency to turbo frequency if Auto mode is selected.When Manual mode is selected,Frequency is just the selected Frequency.

Please select frequency between Base and Turbo,Unit:100MHz.When the input value is less than the base value, the setting is made at the base frequency. When the input value is greater than the maximum supported turbo value, the setting is made at the maximum turbo frequency.

MSR 1FCh Bit[30] = PCH_NEG_DISABLE
On-Die Thermal Throttling
When a processor thermal sensor trips (either core), the PROCHOT# will be driven.  If bi-direction is enabled, external agents can drive PROCHOT# to throttle the processor.
Enable/Disable Thermal Monitor

Controls the CPU response to an inbound platform assertion of xxPROCHOT# by capping to the programmed ratio. Default value 0 will allow ME to control this value. If ME does not set ratio, default 0 equates to Pn. A non-zero value will override ME setting. The min allowed ratio is defined by PLATFORM_INFO[MIN_OPERATING_RATIO]
Offset from factory set TCC activation temperature at which the Thermal Control Circuit must be activated.
